text:
This paper provides a comprehensive mechanistic investigation into anti-Markovnikov alkene hydrofunctionalization reactions catalyzed by 9-mesityl-10-methylacridinium (Mes-Acr+). Utilizing a combination of transient absorption spectroscopy, steady-state fluorescence, and density functional theory (DFT) calculations, the authors elucidate the complex interplay between the photoredox catalyst and hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) cocatalysts such as thiophenol and diphenyl disulfide. 

Laser flash photolysis enabled the direct detection of alkene cation radical intermediates, confirming a single-electron transfer mechanism over alternative exciplex pathways. The study identifies that while all studied alkenes are oxidized by the singlet excited state of Mes-Acr+ on a nanosecond timescale, those with lower oxidation potentials may also involve the triplet state. A vital finding is the high-speed oxidation of the persistent acridinyl radical (Mes-Acr·) by the phenylthiyl radical, a step that effectively couples the photoredox and HAT catalytic cycles. 

Comparative analysis reveals that diphenyl disulfide functions as a pre-catalyst that undergoes direct photolytic cleavage to enter the same catalytic manifold as thiophenol. DFT results suggests that thiophenol is a significantly more efficient HAT donor than 2-phenylmalononitrile due to lower structural reorganization energies. Finally, the observation of a reversible, off-cycle acridinium-thiolate adduct during the reaction suggests that proton transfer may be a key rate-limiting factor in these transformations. These insights provide a robust framework for the future design and optimization of acridinium-based photocatalytic systems.
